orchestrator_notes: RT-6 / Enhancement #42. Failed and blocked agents display identically on dashboard — both show as 'need input'. Should visually distinguish terminal (failed) vs recoverable (blocked) states. Trace the full flow: agent status in DB -> API response -> Vue dashboard rendering. May be non-issue for UI (report suggests investigating first). P2 severity, E1 effort.

status_values.note: Frontend also recognizes 'handed_over' (added in Handover 0506) but this is not in the DB constraint

failed_history.migration_action: All 'failed' records migrated to 'blocked' (failure_reason copied to block_reason). All 'cancelled' records migrated to 'decommissioned'. failure_reason column dropped entirely.

workflow_status_fields.agents: per-agent AgentWorkflowDetail list with raw status string

api_response.note: WorkflowStatus docstring: 'Removed: failed_agents, cancelled_agents (replaced by blocked/silent/decommissioned)'

frontend_mapping.unused_css_variable: --status-failure: #c6298c (defined in agent-colors.scss line 58, never referenced anywhere)

findings.root_cause: There is NO 'failed' status in the system. It was explicitly removed in Handover 0491 (migration b8d2f3a4e567, Feb 2026). All errors unconditionally set status to 'blocked' via report_error(). The original issue RT-6/#42 assumes both 'failed' and 'blocked' exist and display identically — but 'failed' does not exist at all. There is only ONE error state ('blocked'), so there is nothing to differentiate. The real gap is that the system conflates terminal failures (agent crashed, unrecoverable) with recoverable blocks (agent needs orchestrator input) into a single 'blocked' status.

proposed_fix.approach: Reintroduce 'failed' as a distinct terminal status, separate from 'blocked' (recoverable)

backend_changes.what: Add 'failed' to the AgentExecution CHECK constraint
Add conditional logic to report_error() — distinguish recoverable vs terminal errors
backend_changes.detail: If error is terminal (e.g. agent crashed, max retries exceeded, fatal exception), set status='failed'. If error is recoverable (needs input, rate limited, temporary issue), keep status='blocked'. Could use a 'fatal' boolean param or infer from error type.

backend_changes.what: Add failed_agents count to WorkflowStatus schema

frontend_changes.what: Add 'failed' entry to statusConfig.js (both statusConfig and STATUS_CONFIG objects)
frontend_changes.detail: failed: { label: 'Failed', color: '#c6298c', italic: false, chipColor: 'error' } — reuse the existing unused --status-failure CSS variable color
failed: { icon: 'mdi-alert-circle', color: 'error', label: 'Failed', description: 'Agent encountered a terminal error' }
failed: 1 (same priority as blocked, or higher at 0.5 to surface failures first)
Add 'failed' to the validator includes array

semantic_distinction.blocked: Recoverable — agent needs orchestrator/user input to continue. Action: send a message to unblock.
semantic_distinction.failed: Terminal — agent crashed, hit fatal error, can't recover on its own. Action: investigate, respawn, or mark as decommissioned.

sessions.notes_for_next: The fix requires changes across all 3 layers (DB migration, API schema, Vue frontend). The key design decision for 0803b is: how does report_error() decide between 'blocked' and 'failed'? Options: (1) Add a 'fatal: bool' parameter to report_error() and let the agent decide, (2) Infer from error type/category, (3) Add a separate report_failure() method. The unused CSS variable --status-failure: #c6298c is already defined and ready to use for the frontend color. Also consider: should 'failed' agents be auto-decommissioned after a timeout, or stay visible for investigation?
sessions.summary: 'Failed' is NOT a real status — it was removed in Handover 0491 (Feb 2026). All errors set status='blocked' unconditionally. The system has no concept of terminal failure vs recoverable block. The frontend correctly maps 'blocked' to 'Needs Input' (orange) but has no 'failed' entry at all. Root cause: the status simplification in 0491 went too far by collapsing failed+blocked into one state. Fix requires reintroducing 'failed' across backend (DB constraint + report_error logic), API (WorkflowStatus schema), and frontend (statusConfig.js + CSS).

sessions.title: Implement: Status Display Fix

Add conditional logic to report_error() — fatal param or separate report_failure() method
Add failed_agents to WorkflowStatus schema in service_responses.py
Update get_workflow_status() to count failed agents
Add 'failed' entry to statusConfig.js (both config objects)
Add 'failed' to AGENT_STATUS_PRIORITY in constants.js
Add 'failed' to StatusChip.vue validator
Add .status-failed CSS class to LaunchTab.vue
Update agent-colors.scss if needed (--status-failure already exists)
Run existing tests to verify no regressions
Add tests for new 'failed' status transitions

chain_summary: Chain closed as BY DESIGN. 'Failed' status was intentionally removed in Handover 0491 (Feb 2026). All errors go to 'blocked' status. The 0491 simplification was a deliberate architectural decision — reintroducing 'failed' would require DB migration + backend + frontend changes for minimal benefit. Orchestrators already handle blocked agents via the CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED remediation protocol (0800b).
